doc_description: This document provides essential dietary strategies and lifestyle modifications for individuals using GLP-1 medications, focusing on meal timing, nausea management, digestive health, protein intake, muscle maintenance, vitamin preservation, and foods to avoid, to enhance medication effectiveness and minimize side effects.

structure.title: GLP-1 and Diet
structure.summary:
The partial document discusses the importance of diet and lifestyle modifications for individuals taking GLP-1 medications such as Zepbound, Ozempic, Wegovy, and Saxenda. It emphasizes that these medications can help control blood sugar, reduce hunger, and aid in weight loss, but a healthy diet is crucial for their effectiveness and to minimize side effects. Key points include:

1. **Meal Timing**: Recommendations for small, frequent meals every 3-4 hours to prevent hypoglycemia and manage nausea.
2. **Nausea Management**: Suggestions for alleviating nausea through diet, including adequate fluids and avoiding lying down after meals.
3. **Digestive Health**: Tips for managing constipation and diarrhea, emphasizing fiber intake, hydration, and physical activity.
4. **Protein Intake**: Guidance on protein consumption to support muscle repair and growth, with a focus on lean and plant-based sources.
5. **Muscle Maintenance**: Encouragement to incorporate muscle-strengthening exercises and allow for recovery time.
6. **Vitamin Preservation**: Advice on preventing vitamin loss during weight loss by maintaining a varied diet rich in fruits, vegetables, and healthy fats.
7. **Foods to Avoid**: A list of foods that may cause discomfort, including spicy, high-sodium, gas-producing, and fried foods. 

Overall, the document provides a comprehensive overview of dietary strategies to enhance the effectiveness of GLP-1 medications while addressing potential side effects.
structure.text:
GLP- 1 and Diet 
 
Medicines like Zepbound , Ozempic, Wegovy , and Saxenda  help control blood sugar, make you feel less 
hungry, and can help with weight loss.  
Eating healthy while taking these medicines is important to help them work better and to avoid side 
effects like feeling sick or having an upset stomach.   
 
Diet and lifestyle tips to support GLP -1 medication:  
Timing of Meals
 • Small, frequent mea ls will only make you feel better . 
• Aim for a meal every 3 -4 hours.  
• Will help  reduce hypoglycemia  if you are taking other  diabetes medications.  
Nause a 
 • Small, frequent meals and adequate ﬂuids help with nausea.  
• You can also try ginger or peppermint tea.  
• Getting enough ﬂuids if you are throwing up is important to avoid any kidney 
injury.  
• Avoid lying down after meals for 2 -3 hours.  
• Avoid fried foods.  
Constipation  
and Diarrhea  
 • Getting enough ﬁber from fruits, vegetables, and whole grains with help with 
diarrhea . NOTE:  Be careful about potassium if you are watching that.  
• Be active each day.  
• Drink plenty of ﬂuids, especially water. NOTE: some kidney patients have ﬂuid 
limits - check with your doctor to make sure.  
Protei n 
 • Protein goals are similar to a kidney  diet .  
• Protein helps to grow and repair muscle that you lose while losing weight. Aim for lean meats, ﬁsh, and plant- based proteins such as beans.  
• Spread protein through the day . 
• Work with your dietitian to understand what you need.  
Muscle Loss 
 • Incorporate muscle exercises 2 -3 times per week. This includes squats, lunges, 
and push- ups.  
• If you can lift weights , you can use a resistance band while sitting down.  
• Rest 48 hours between exercise to give muscles time to rebuild.  
Vitamin Loss  
 • Losing weight rapidly can lead to vitamin loss.  
• To help you get enough vitamins , it important you have a lot of variation in 
your diet.  
• Choose foods from fruits, vegetables, healthy fats, and lean proteins.  
• Limit  empty calories such as alcohol, sugars, processed foods, and sweets.  
Avoid  
 • Spicy foods  – Causes heartburn  
• High -sodium  foods  – increases swelling and bloating  
• Gas producing foods  – Broccoli , cauliﬂower, onions, cabbage, and carbonated  
drinks cause d iscomfort  
• Fried and greasy foods  – Causes heartburn  and discomfort

structure.title: GLP-1 and Weight Loss
structure.summary: The partial document discusses the use of GLP-1 medications for weight loss and emphasizes the importance of understanding healthy food groups. It outlines four main food groups: fruits and vegetables, whole grains, lean proteins, and healthy fats, detailing examples of each and their health benefits. Additionally, the document provides tips for healthy eating habits while on GLP-1 medication, including mindful eating, self-reflection before eating, and eating slowly to enhance the overall eating experience and support weight loss goals. It concludes by advising readers to consult a dietitian for personalized dietary guidance.
structure.text:
GLP -1 and Weight Loss 
When using GLP -1 medications to loose weight, you will want to also understand some basic food 
groups and why they are good for you.  
Food Group  Examples  Why It’s Good for You  
Fruits & 
Vegetables  Apples, oranges, berries, spinach, kale  Give your body vitamins, minerals, and ﬁber to stay healthy  
Whole Grains  Brown rice, oatmeal, whole -wheat 
bread, quinoa  Help you feel full and give you energy all 
day  
Lean Proteins  Chicken, ﬁsh, turkey, tofu, tempeh  Help your muscles stay strong and stop 
you from feeling too hungry  
Healthy Fats  Avocados, nuts, seeds, yogurt, olive oil, dark chocolate  Help your heart and brain and can help with weight loss  
 
 Tips for Eating Well  
It is important to build good habits while you are taking GLP -1 medication. This will help you to keep 
healthy and achieve your weight loss goals.  
Tip  What It Means  Why It Helps  
Mindful Eating  Pay attention to what you're eating and 
why you're eating it.  Helps you make better food choices and 
avoid eating when not hungry.  
Ask Yourself First  Before grabbing more food, ask: "Am I 
really hungry or just bored, sad, or 
stressed?"  Stops you from eating just because of 
feelings or habits.  
Eat Slower  T ake your time while eating —  chew 
slowly and enjoy each bite.  Helps you notice when you're full and 
makes meals more enjoyable.  
  
  
©Strive Health, LLC, 2025  
 This is general  diet  info. Ask a dietitian what’s right for you.
